## Coverage

- Protocols: OpenAI Responses, OpenAI Chat Completions, Claude Messages.
- Modes: non-streaming, streaming SSE, selected multi-turn prompts.
- Block-expected attacks: `missing_guard_toolcall`, `guard_digest_mismatch`, `replay_old_nonce`, `tampered_arguments`, `extra_toolcall`.
- Allow-expected controls: `guard_only`, `clean_text`.
- Secret handling: synthetic secret strings are checked against recorded request bodies, error details, and response bodies.

## Findings

- No missed local-demo cases were detected in this run.
- Streaming rows exercise incremental toolcall assembly by reading and sanitizing SSE bodies before recording final stream observations.
- Multi-turn rows keep prior user/assistant context plus sensitive-looking strings to check that guard injection and string protection remain scoped.
- The report stores hashes and summaries only; full request/response payloads are kept out of Markdown to reduce accidental leakage.

## Residual Risks

- The local demo simulates upstream poison formats deterministically; it does not replace real-provider long soak tests.
- The guard digest mismatch family intentionally uses invalid fake digests, so successful blocking proves gateway validation rather than model compliance.
- Timing metrics are local-loop metrics and should not be used as production latency baselines.
